title: Vakratunda Mahakaya
description: A classic beginning prayer—recite before any new task to invoke Ganesha’s blessings and remove obstacles.

text.label: Shloka
text.sanskrit:
वक्रतुण्ड महाकाय सूर्यकोटि समप्रभ।
निर्विघ्नं कुरु मे देव सर्वकार्येषु सर्वदा॥
text.transliteration:
Vakratunda Mahakaya Suryakoti Samaprabha
Nirvighnam Kuru Me Deva Sarvakaryeshu Sarvada
text.translation: O Lord with the curved trunk and mighty body, radiant like millions of suns—make my path free of obstacles in all that I do.

meaning: Invoke this before work, travel, studies, meetings, or any new beginning. Even one recitation with full attention is considered auspicious.

title: Gayatri Mantra
description: A prayer for divine illumination and clarity—traditionally recited at sunrise, noon, and sunset.

text.label: The Mantra
text.sanskrit:
ॐ भूर्भुवः स्वः
तत्सवितुर्वरेण्यं
भर्गो देवस्य धीमहि
धियो यो नः प्रचोदयात्॥
text.transliteration:
Om Bhur Bhuvah Svaha
Tat Savitur Varenyam
Bhargo Devasya Dhimahi
Dhiyo Yo Nah Prachodayat
text.translation: We meditate on the divine radiance of the Supreme—may it illuminate our intellect and guide our understanding.

meaning: If you do japa, start with 11 or 27 repetitions. Consistency matters more than a big number on day one.

title: Maha Mrityunjaya Mantra
description: A powerful prayer for protection, strength, and healing—often recited in difficult times.

text.label: The Mantra
text.sanskrit:
ॐ त्र्यम्बकं यजामहे
सुगन्धिं पुष्टिवर्धनम्।
उर्वारुकमिव बन्धनान्
मृत्योर्मुक्षीय माऽमृतात्॥
text.transliteration:
Om Tryambakam Yajamahe
Sugandhim Pushtivardhanam
Urvarukamiva Bandhanan
Mrityor Mukshiya Maamritat
text.translation: We worship the three-eyed Lord, fragrant and life-nourishing—liberate us from bondage like a ripe fruit from its stem, not from immortality.

meaning: Recite slowly, with steady breath. In tradition, it’s paired with offering water to Shiva (even a simple glass is fine).

title: Asatoma Sadgamaya
description: A timeless prayer: from ignorance to truth, darkness to light, fear to immortality.

text.label: Prayer
text.sanskrit:
असतो मा सद्गमय।
तमसो मा ज्योतिर्गमय।
मृत्योर्मा अमृतं गमय॥
ॐ शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः॥
text.transliteration:
Asato Ma Sadgamaya
Tamaso Ma Jyotirgamaya
Mrityor Ma Amritam Gamaya
Om Shantih Shantih Shantih
text.translation: Lead me from untruth to truth, darkness to light, and death to immortality. Peace, peace, peace.

meaning: A perfect closing prayer after study, meditation, or at the end of the day.

title: Shanti Mantra (Sahana Vavatu)
description: Traditionally chanted before learning or spiritual study to invoke harmony between teacher and student.

text.label: Shanti Mantra
text.sanskrit:
ॐ सह नाववतु।
सह नौ भुनक्तु।
सह वीर्यं करवावहै।
तेजस्वि नावधीतमस्तु।
मा विद्विषावहै॥
ॐ शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः॥
text.transliteration:
Om Saha Navavatu
Saha Nau Bhunaktu
Saha Viryam Karavavahai
Tejasvi Navadhitamastu
Ma Vidvishavahai
Om Shantih Shantih Shantih
text.translation: May we be protected together, nourished together, and work together with strength. May our learning be radiant; may we never oppose each other. Peace, peace, peace.

meaning: Great before studying scripture, reading Gita, or even starting a focused work session.

title: Om Namah Shivaya
description: A simple mantra with deep power—centering, purifying, and calming.

text.label: Mantra
text.translation: Salutations to Shiva (the auspicious, the inner Self).

meaning: If you’re new, do 3 minutes daily. Let the breath carry the sound gently.

title: Saraswati Vandana (Ya Kundendu)
description: A beloved prayer for wisdom, clarity, and learning—perfect before study or creative work.

text.label: Stotra
text.sanskrit:
या कुन्देन्दु तुषारहारधवला या शुभ्रवस्त्रावृता।
या वीणावरदण्डमण्डितकरा या श्वेतपद्मासना॥
या ब्रह्माच्युतशंकरप्रभृतिभिर्देवैः सदा वन्दिता।
सा मां पातु सरस्वती भगवती निःशेषजाड्यापहा॥
text.transliteration:
Ya Kundendu Tusharahara Dhavala Ya Shubhra Vastra Avrita
Ya Veena Varadanda Mandita Kara Ya Shveta Padmasana
Ya Brahmachyuta Shankara Prabhritibhir Devaih Sada Vandita
Sa Mam Patu Saraswati Bhagavati Nihshesha Jadyapaha
text.translation: O Goddess Saraswati—pure and radiant, worshipped by the gods—protect me and remove all dullness and ignorance.

meaning: Great for students, exams, interviews, writing, music practice, and any knowledge work.

title: Lakshmi Mantra (Om Shreem Mahalakshmyai Namah)
description: A simple mantra for gratitude, steadiness, and abundance—material and spiritual.

text.label: Mantra
text.translation: Salutations to Goddess Mahalakshmi.

meaning: Pair with gratitude practice: 3 things you’re thankful for after chanting.
